analogClaims.claim: lives in the menu bar, unobtrusive during work and calls
analogClaims.source: https://macmenubar.com/breathepulse/ ; Mindfulness Bell Menu Bar (App Store)

analogClaims.claim: guides breathing with visual + audio + haptic cues (trackpad haptics on Mac)
offers box breathing 4-4-4-4 plus customizable inhale/exhale/pause durations
analogClaims.source: Awesome Breathing: Pacer Timer (App Store); BreathePulse

analogClaims.claim: remembers last settings and starts immediately with a big control
Apple Health integration for mindful minutes

baselineRequirements: menu-bar presence, unobtrusive — never steals focus
box breathing 4-4-4-4 as default, with customizable durations
minimalist visual guide synced to the breath phases
audio cue (toggleable) and trackpad haptic cue
one-click start from the menu bar
customDelta: optimized for focus during work: short sessions that run during calls without disruption
successCriteria: user starts a session in one click from the menu bar
session runs without stealing focus from the active app
visual/audio/haptic cues stay in sync with the chosen pattern
acceptanceTests: clicking the menu-bar icon then Start shows a guide animating on a 4-4-4-4 cadence
toggling sound off removes audio while visual and haptic cues continue
quitting and relaunching restores the last pattern and preferences
dataFlows: user preferences read/written locally; no data leaves the machine by default
localStorage: selected pattern, custom durations, sound/haptic preferences
unknowns: does the user want daily reminders?
does the user want Apple Health sync for mindful minutes?

decisionLog.decision: minimalist visual guide with a dark, quiet menu-bar UI
decisionLog.rationale: UI/UX detail — best-in-class for an unobtrusive focus tool; decided for the user

decisionLog.decision: audio off by default, haptics on

decisionLog.rationale: common pattern but touches the notifications permission

decisionLog.decision: Apple Health sync for mindful minutes
decisionLog.rationale: touches health data — high risk; only with explicit consent

unresolvedRisks: Apple Health is health data — include only with explicit user consent
